title: How NVIDIA engineers and researchers build with Codex
optimized_headline: Inside NVIDIA: Engineers Reveal Their Secrets to Using Codex Effectively
raw_excerpt: Teams use Codex with GPT-5.5 to ship production systems and turn research ideas into runnable experiments.
raw_body: Teams use Codex with GPT-5.5 to ship production systems and turn research ideas into runnable experiments.
speedrun: NVIDIA engineers are leveraging Codex alongside GPT-5.5 to streamline the development of production systems and transform research concepts into practical experiments. This collaboration enhances efficiency, allowing teams to rapidly prototype and deploy solutions. The integration of these advanced tools could significantly reduce development time and increase innovation within the company. This shift matters now as it positions NVIDIA at the forefront of AI-driven engineering practices.
why_it_matters: NVIDIA engineers could see faster project turnaround, enabling them to focus on innovation rather than routine coding tasks.
This collaboration signals a broader trend in the tech industry toward integrating AI tools for enhanced productivity and efficiency.

lenses.eli12: NVIDIA's engineers are using a smart coding assistant called Codex with a powerful AI, GPT-5.5, to make their work easier. Imagine having a helpful guide that speeds up your homework; that's what Codex does for coding. This is important because it allows engineers to spend more time on creative ideas and less on repetitive tasks, which could lead to better technology for everyone.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, the use of Codex with GPT-5.5 could mean faster product development cycles. By automating routine coding tasks, teams can allocate resources to more strategic initiatives. This approach not only meets user needs more efficiently but also reduces costs associated with lengthy development processes.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, integrating Codex with GPT-5.5 allows NVIDIA engineers to rapidly prototype and iterate on their projects. This combination enhances the coding process, enabling quicker experimentation and deployment of models. However, teams must ensure that the outputs are thoroughly validated to maintain quality and performance standards.
